title: At The Fair (The Rocky Road To Dublin)
content:
The lark shall never come to say
        To a gombeen-man, "Good day,"
        And the lark shall never cry
        To a kindly man, "Good-bye."

        See the greedy gombeen-man
        Taking everything he can
        From man and woman, dog and cat, 
        And the lark does not like that.
meaning:
James Stephens's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "At The Fair (The Rocky Road To Dublin)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
